etymology.description: 止(止) (zhǐ, "foot") + 凵(凵) (kǎn, "cave") = to step out of a cave -> to exit
etymology.interpretation: The original depiction involved a foot 止(止) (zhǐ, "foot") stepping out of a cave 凵(凵) (kǎn, "cave"), representing the act of exiting or going out. This visual concept naturally conveys the meaning of leaving an enclosed space.

examples.chinese: 我每天早上出门(我每天早上出門)。
他出去了(他出去了)，给我留下了一封信(給我留下了一封信)。
examples.pinyin: Tā chūqù le, gěi wǒ liúxià le yī fēng xìn.
examples.english: He went out and left me a letter.

examples.chinese: 晚上出门(晚上出門)需要小心(需要小心)。
examples.english: Going out at night requires caution.
